l—all these sacrificial materials should be put into full
pots, and the same collected materials offered properly to the Deities
as Bali (food) along with eatables of various kinds, consisting mostly
of honey, milk porridge and barley preparations.
[In some editions in the last verse is read as tt^, thus
making it an adjective of In the first verse ^uJywtiPM- is the
other reading. A big list of herbs—Osadhis—is given here. They are
to be put into the Holy Pot which is worshipped. The Balyupaharana
may also mean the offering of food to the different Deities on the
ground, and not necessarily offerings to the fire.]

Lustration Ceremony XLIV 365

The sacrificial twigs required are of Kbadira, Pala^a, Indian
fig tree, Ka^marl and Asvattha (holy fig tree). One who wishes for
prosperity should use the sacrificial ladle made of gold or silver.
